archetype: Automated Hydroponic Crop Yield Optimization and Nutrient Dosing System for Large-Scale Vertical Farms
description: This system optimizes crop yields and nutrient dosing for large-scale vertical farms, utilizing machine learning algorithms and real-time data from sensors and weather forecasts. It is used by vertical farm operators to maximize crop growth and minimize resource waste. The system must integrate with a variety of hardware and software systems, including climate control systems, LED grow lights, and nutrient delivery systems.
rules: MUST integrate with ASHRAE 90.1-2019 climate control standards
MUST adhere to ISO 16119:2013 for hydroponic crop nutrient management
MUST support integration with industry-standard LED grow light protocols such as DALI
MUST handle variable sensor data formats from different manufacturers
MUST incorporate real-time weather forecasting data from sources such as the National Weather Service
anti_patterns: DO NOT rely solely on historical data for predictions, as it may not account for changing environmental conditions
DO NOT neglect to consider the unique growth characteristics of different crop species
DO NOT fail to account for the impact of variable lighting conditions on crop growth
key_technical_challenges: Developing machine learning models that can accurately predict crop yields based on complex environmental factors
Integrating with a variety of hardware and software systems from different manufacturers
Handling variable sensor data formats and frequencies

recommended_stack.language: Python + due to its extensive libraries for machine learning and data analysis
recommended_stack.framework: Flask + for building a scalable and modular API
recommended_stack.database_engine: PostgreSQL + for handling complex queries and sensor data
recommended_stack.cache: Redis + for storing frequently accessed data and reducing database queries
recommended_stack.message_broker: RabbitMQ + for handling real-time data from sensors and weather forecasts
